title: Bomb Threat Response (Code Black)

contact_extensions.national_center: National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management

plan_procedures.action: When the security operations room receives a report of a bomb threat, it must rapidly verify the source of the report and collect the initial available information related to the threat.

Immediately notify the relevant specialized authorities through the approved emergency numbers and communicate with facility leadership to determine activation of Code Black through the internal overhead speaker system.
Strictly follow all directions and technical guidance issued by specialists responsible for this type of incident in order to reduce potential risks.
Monitor all possible locations related to the reported threat according to the initial information available, using the closed-circuit television monitoring system.
Ensure that security personnel moving toward the suspected site do not use wired or wireless communication devices in order to avoid triggering potential risks.
Warn all security personnel not to attempt moving, examining, opening, or approaching suspicious objects until specialists arrive and assess the scene.
Establish a perimeter around the suspected area, prevent persons from entering it, and maintain site isolation until specialists begin their direct tasks.
Execute evacuation of patients, staff, and others in the facility when directed by incident command and according to the severity and location of the threat.
Coordinate and cooperate with specialized authorities to facilitate incident handling procedures in accordance with the approved protocol.
Continue tracking security updates, operational information, and all incident developments and relay them to the relevant leadership and response authorities.
Permit the resumption of activity in the incident area only after the event has ended and formal approval has been issued by the relevant specialists.
Prepare and submit a joint incident report with the relevant specialists in the facility and the specialized security authorities to facility leadership.
Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required information related to the incident through the responsible authority in the facility.

learning_objectives: Verify and escalate a bomb threat using the correct Code Black procedure.
Coordinate communication with specialized authorities and facility leadership while applying communication restrictions near the suspected area.
Use monitoring, perimeter control, and suspicious object safety rules to reduce risk.
Support evacuation decisions and operational coordination with specialists during the incident.
Complete tracking, reporting, and external notification requirements after the event.
